notable_pick_trades: Traded 2026 1st-round pick (No. 13 overall) to Los Angeles Rams in 2025 draft to move up and select EDGE James Pearce Jr. at No. 26. Currently hold only 5 picks: R2 (48), R3 (79), R4 (114), R6 (195), R7 (231). GM Ian Cunningham has stated he wants to acquire more picks via trades.
roster_context: Massive organizational overhaul: fired HC Raheem Morris and GM Terry Fontenot after back-to-back 8-9 seasons. Hired Matt Ryan as President of Football, Kevin Stefanski as HC, Ian Cunningham as GM, and Tommy Rees as OC. QB Michael Penix Jr. tore his ACL in Week 11 and is aiming for a Week 1 return but uncertain. Kirk Cousins being released with $35M dead cap. Cutting WR Darnell Mooney. Kyle Pitts franchise tagged. Kaden Elliss likely leaving in free agency. James Pearce Jr. arrested on 5 felony charges (domestic violence) in February, status uncertain. No first-round pick severely limits draft capital. Over half the roster hitting free agency.

needs.explanation: Cutting Darnell Mooney ($18.4M cap hit) leaves Drake London as the only proven receiver on the roster. Mooney was terrible in 2025 after a collarbone injury in training camp. Behind London there is virtually nothing — David Sills V is a free agent, depth was described as 'absurd' in how thin it was. Cunningham called WR a 'premium position' at the combine. This team desperately needs a WR2.
needs.depth: Extremely thin after London. Dylan Drummond, Nathan Carter (injured), and practice squad-level players only.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2 (No. 48) — top priority for first pick

needs.explanation: James Pearce Jr. faces 5 felony charges including aggravated battery and aggravated stalking after a February arrest. His status for 2026 is in serious doubt — potential NFL suspension and legal proceedings could sideline him. Leonard Floyd is a UFA likely to depart. Arnold Ebiketie is also a UFA and seemed frustrated with his role. Jalon Walker (6 sacks as a rookie) is the only locked-in edge piece. The team set a franchise record with 57 sacks in 2025 but could lose the majority of that production.
needs.current_starter: Jalon Walker, James Pearce Jr. (legal uncertainty)
needs.depth: Leonard Floyd (UFA), Arnold Ebiketie (UFA), DeAngelo Malone, Khalid Kareem (UFA). Significant attrition likely.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-3, or Round 6-7 for depth if FA addresses starting need

needs.explanation: A.J. Terrell is the CB1 anchor, but CB2 Mike Hughes is not a long-term answer despite performing valiantly. Only Terrell and Hughes are under contract beyond 2026, and Hughes could be a cap casualty before 2027. Dee Alford (slot CB, team-best 13 PBUs) is a UFA. Billy Bowman Jr. (4th-round rookie) showed promise in the slot but was placed on IR. The team needs a true CB2 to play opposite Terrell.
needs.current_starter: A.J. Terrell (CB1), Mike Hughes (CB2), Billy Bowman Jr. (slot/IR)
needs.depth: Kevin King (re-signed), Cobee Bryant, Natrone Brooks. Alford likely departing as UFA.

needs.explanation: Jake Matthews turns 34 and is in the twilight of his career — his run blocking declined in 2025 and he had 8 penalties. Kaleb McGary missed the entire 2025 season with a knee injury and could be cut to save $15M. Elijah Wilkinson (UFA) filled in admirably at RT but is a journeyman. The team needs to begin succession planning at tackle, especially with a left-handed QB (Penix) where RT is the blind side.
needs.current_starter: Jake Matthews (LT, age 34), Kaleb McGary (RT, returning from knee injury)
needs.depth: Elijah Wilkinson (UFA), Storm Norton (injured all of 2025), Jack Nelson. Very thin after starters.

needs.explanation: Kaden Elliss — the defensive heartbeat, green-dot wearer, and leading tackler with 100+ tackles in 3 straight seasons — is an UFA and expected to leave. DC Jeff Ulbrich said replacing him 'would take more than one human being.' Troy Andersen is also a UFA with an extensive injury history. Divine Deablo showed flashes but missed time. The off-ball LB group could be gutted by free agency.
needs.current_starter: Kaden Elliss (UFA, likely departing), Divine Deablo
needs.depth: Troy Andersen (UFA), JD Bertrand, Josh Woods (UFA), Ronnie Harrison. Extremely thin if Elliss and Andersen leave.

needs.explanation: David Onyemata is a UFA at age 34 and likely departing. Ruke Orhorhoro and Brandon Dorlus both showed progress as young interior linemen, but depth is lacking, especially at nose tackle. The team was 'light up front' per multiple reports. John Franklin-Myers is a rumored FA target but would cost around $20M. Need a run-stuffing presence to complement the young players.
needs.depth: David Onyemata (UFA), Zach Harrison (IR), Kentavius Street (UFA), Elijah Garcia. Young but thin.

needs.explanation: Chris Lindstrom is elite at RG and locked in. Matthew Bergeron is a developing LG. Ryan Neuzil played well at center but the new regime may want a different, bigger player at the position. Neuzil is adequate but not a long-term lock. OL coach Bill Callahan could elevate the group, but center depth is nonexistent.
needs.current_starter: Matthew Bergeron (LG), Ryan Neuzil (C), Chris Lindstrom (RG)
needs.depth: Kyle Hinton, Jovaughn Gwyn. Center depth is a concern if Neuzil is supplanted.

needs.explanation: Bijan Robinson is an elite RB1. However, Tyler Allgeier is a UFA and likely to depart for a starting job elsewhere. That leaves no proven RB2 behind Robinson. Nathan Carter missed most of 2025 with a knee injury. A Day 3 or cheap FA addition is needed for depth.
needs.depth: Tyler Allgeier (UFA, likely gone), Nathan Carter (injury concerns). Need a backup.

free_agency_impact: CUT Kirk Cousins (post-June 1 designation, $35M dead cap) — must acquire veteran QB via FA or trade; Penix is the intended starter but recovering from ACL
CUT Darnell Mooney ($18.4M cap hit, saving $7.4-11.9M) — WR need skyrockets, only Drake London remains
FRANCHISE TAGGED Kyle Pitts ($15.1M, 1 year) — TE need off the board for 2026, keeps a key weapon
CUT KhaDarel Hodge ($3.3M) — special teams loss
UFA Kaden Elliss (ILB) — likely departing per Cunningham's lukewarm comments; massive LB need created
UFA Leonard Floyd (EDGE) — likely departing; compounds EDGE uncertainty with Pearce legal situation
UFA David Onyemata (DL, age 34) — likely departing; IDL depth thins
UFA Arnold Ebiketie (EDGE) — likely departing; seemed frustrated with role
UFA Tyler Allgeier (RB) — likely departing for starting job; RB2 need created
UFA Dee Alford (CB) — team-best 13 PBUs, strong market; slot CB depth hit
UFA Troy Andersen (ILB) — injury-prone, may not return; further depletes LB group
UFA Elijah Wilkinson (OT) — started every snap at RT in 2025; losing him leaves OT razor thin
RE-SIGNED Kevin King (CB) — minimal impact, depth piece
LINKED to Tua Tagovailoa, Joe Flacco as veteran QB acquisitions via trade/FA
LINKED to John Franklin-Myers (DL) as potential FA signing (~$20M AAV)
scheme_fit_notes: Kevin Stefanski brings a heavy play-action, under-center offense with wide-zone run concepts — Bijan Robinson is the centerpiece. OC Tommy Rees ran more in-breaking routes in Cleveland. WR prospects who win on intermediate/slot routes and provide YAC fit better than pure deep threats. On defense, Jeff Ulbrich's aggressive scheme blitzed at the 2nd-highest rate in the NFL in 2025. Edge rushers who can set the edge in the run game AND rush the passer are prioritized. Off-ball LBs with blitz ability are highly valued (Elliss was an elite blitzing LB). The 4-3 under front means IDL prospects need to be versatile enough to play multiple alignments. CB2 must be able to play man coverage to hold up when the defense sends extra rushers.
